title: Unit Converter
description: Convert length, weight, temperature, area, volume, and speed units. Supports metric and imperial. Free online tool.
hint: Enter a value above to see all conversions.

howToUse.label: Select a category
howToUse.description: Choose from Length, Weight, Temperature, Area, Volume, or Speed using the category buttons at the top.

howToUse.label: Enter a value and select the source unit
howToUse.description: Type the number you want to convert in the input field, then choose the source unit from the dropdown.

howToUse.label: View all conversions at once
howToUse.description: All available units for the selected category are displayed simultaneously, so you can compare values across different systems at a glance.

howToUse.label: Copy a result
howToUse.description: Click the Copy button next to any unit to copy that value to your clipboard.

faq.question: What unit systems are supported?
faq.answer: This tool supports both the metric (SI) system and the imperial/US customary system. For example, for length you can convert between millimeters, centimeters, meters, kilometers, inches, feet, yards, and miles.

faq.question: How accurate are the conversions?
faq.answer: All conversions use standard, widely accepted conversion factors (e.g. 1 inch = 0.0254 meters exactly). Results are displayed with up to 8 significant figures.

faq.question: Why is temperature converted differently from other units?
faq.answer: Unlike most units, temperature scales (Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin) use different zero points, so conversion requires both multiplication and addition rather than a simple ratio. For example, 0°C = 32°F = 273.15 K.

faq.question: What are US gallons vs. imperial gallons?
faq.answer: This tool uses US gallons (approximately 3.785 liters). The imperial (UK) gallon is different (approximately 4.546 liters). Similarly, US fluid ounces, cups, pints, and quarts are US customary measurements.
